subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Analyse how the mean changes when values are added or removed
Understand the balancing property of mean
Calculate median in datasets with even and odd number of observations
Use frequency tables to compute mean and median
Use spreadsheets to compute totals and averages
Interpret patterns and trends using line graphs
Apply statistical concepts to real-life datasets

lesson_plan.title: Exploring the Mean with Multiple Data Sets
topics_covered: Mean as the center of data
Mean in datasets with more than two numbers
student_activities: Calculate mean for different sets of numbers

lesson_plan.title: Balancing Property of the Mean
student_activities: Compute distances of numbers from the mean

lesson_plan.title: Mean Changes with Data Modification
student_activities: Add and remove values in datasets

lesson_plan.title: Median in Complex Data Sets
topics_covered: Median for even number of observations

lesson_plan.title: Mean and Median with Frequency Tables
Line Graph Analysis and Revision
student_activities: Draw line graph from rainfall data

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyse patterns rather than only calculate values, so they understand how mean and median behave in real data.
